title: A Bicyclops Built For Two

jokes.text: Several years ago, I tried to log on to AOL, and it just went through!
jokes.setup: Professor announces good news about getting online
jokes.punchline: AOL taking several years to connect subverts expectation of instant future tech
jokes.explanation: Classic Professor absurdism - future tech being worse than present. Well-integrated with character's tendency toward nonsensical announcements.

jokes.text: It smells like burning monkey. Oh? I guess when you're around it all day, you stop noticing.
jokes.setup: Fry comments on terrible smell of net suits
jokes.punchline: Professor's casual admission he's accustomed to burning monkey smell
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered with perfect casual tone. Economy is excellent - gets maximum disturbing comedy from minimal setup.

jokes.text: My God! It's full of ads!
jokes.setup: Dramatic reveal of the Internet
jokes.punchline: Parodies '2001: A Space Odyssey' but about internet advertising
jokes.explanation: Solid reference joke that works on multiple levels - parodies classic sci-fi while commenting on internet reality.

jokes.text: That's me! No, it isn't! I just took some pictures of your face and put them on someone else's body.
jokes.setup: Leela thinks she sees herself online
jokes.punchline: Bender casually admits to creating fake pornographic images of her
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender moment - casually admitting to something horrible. Good structure with misdirection.

jokes.text: $9.95 a minute. You're a dollar naughtier than most.
jokes.setup: Naughty nurse charges premium rate
jokes.punchline: Bender quantifies naughtiness in dollar terms
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that fits Bender's logical-but-crude perspective on monetizing everything.

jokes.text: How about them Knicks?
jokes.setup: Fry enters chat room expecting sexy conversation
jokes.punchline: Immediately pivots to sports small talk
jokes.explanation: Classic Fry social awkwardness - perfectly in character but not particularly original.

jokes.text: A woman? I'm scared! Thanks to the Internet, I'm bored with sex. Is the space pope reptilian?
jokes.setup: Characters react to finding a woman online
jokes.punchline: Escalating from scared to bored to completely random space pope question
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with perfect character voices. 'Space pope reptilian' became a fan-favorite quotable line.

jokes.text: We call it a video game. Video game, you say? Well, golly gee! You mighty spacemen of the future will have to show me how it works.
jokes.setup: Professor explains video games to Fry
jokes.punchline: Fry sarcastically plays dumb about something from his own time period
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Fry's occasional self-awareness about his temporal displacement.

jokes.text: The doctor is in! The doctor is out.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg announces he's available for consultation
jokes.punchline: Immediately reverses position, presumably due to violence
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective Zoidberg physical comedy. Economy is excellent but concept is predictable.

jokes.text: Gotcha! I won! I'm the greatest!
jokes.setup: Fry appears to accidentally kill Alcazar while Leela is devastated
jokes.punchline: Reveals he was just playing a video game, completely oblivious to context
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry obliviousness moment. Excellent misdirection and character integration - only Fry would be this tone-deaf.

jokes.text: Kicked your ass.
jokes.setup: Alcazar sends emotional message about being shot
jokes.punchline: Fry's proud, casual interjection about his gaming victory
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and character work. Two words that perfectly capture Fry's oblivious pride.

jokes.text: Too bad it's a dump.
jokes.setup: Leela expresses emotional significance of finding her home planet
jokes.punchline: Bender immediately deflates the moment with blunt assessment
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender - perfectly timed deflation of emotional moment. Excellent economy.

jokes.text: Lazy sculptor.
jokes.setup: Leela notices statue has one eye like her
jokes.punchline: Fry assumes it's a mistake rather than cultural significance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry logic - missing obvious cultural context. Good misdirection from meaningful to mundane.

jokes.text: Are you real, or am I seeing single?
jokes.setup: Leela encounters another one-eyed being
jokes.punchline: Plays on 'seeing double' but inverted for one-eyed perspective
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character-specific wordplay. Only Leela could make this joke - perfect integration of her unique physiology.

jokes.text: After all this time, somebody else with one eye who isn't a clumsy carpenter or a kid with a BB gun.
jokes.setup: Alcazar expresses joy at meeting another cyclops
jokes.punchline: Distinguishes from mundane causes of one-eyedness
jokes.explanation: Solid observational humor about common causes of eye loss. Well-constructed but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Any artwork of her from the back?
jokes.setup: Alcazar shows sacred mosaic of beauty goddess
jokes.punchline: Bender immediately sexualizes the religious artwork
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender crudeness. Perfect character integration but the concept is predictable for him.

jokes.text: Is that like a cemetery? Because I have to pee.
jokes.setup: Alcazar mentions the Forbidden Valley where no one may tread
jokes.punchline: Fry's practical bladder concerns override sacred taboo
jokes.explanation: Good Fry moment - completely missing spiritual significance for basic bodily needs. Nice misdirection.

jokes.text: Of course. If anything, I'm more impressed
jokes.setup: Alcazar apologizes for living in a giant castle
jokes.punchline: Leela's dry response reveals the absurdity of apologizing for luxury
jokes.explanation: Solid deadpan delivery highlighting the false modesty. Good but not exceptional.

jokes.text: I'm gonna need to make some room!
jokes.setup: Bender sees all the luxury items in the castle
jokes.punchline: Immediately plans to steal everything, visual gag of him assessing loot
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender character moment - immediately calculating theft opportunities. High character integration.

jokes.text: How far away, do you think? A trillion miles?
jokes.setup: Alcazar asks Leela to guess distance of missile attack
jokes.punchline: Comically large number that makes targeting impossible
jokes.explanation: Absurd distance makes the targeting illogical, but joke lacks strong setup or character specificity.

jokes.text: Alcazar, I used to be a baby. It might have been me!
jokes.setup: Alcazar tells story of one baby being saved
jokes.punchline: Leela's overly obvious observation about her own past
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Leela's earnest logical thinking leading to obvious conclusion.

jokes.text: I'm still not following you.
jokes.setup: Leela explains they're male and female and could continue their race
jokes.punchline: Alcazar's feigned ignorance about reproduction
jokes.explanation: Standard 'playing dumb about sex' joke. Functional but relies on obvious comedic crutch.

jokes.text: Yeah, they'll come in handy if I need to cover any tiny manholes.
jokes.setup: Alcazar compliments Leela's pancakes
jokes.punchline: Fry's brutal honesty about their concrete-like quality
jokes.explanation: Excellent Fry moment - his blunt honesty cutting through social politeness. Specific visual metaphor works well.

jokes.text: Leela's experiencing a woman's greatest joy, worshiping a lowlife jerk.
jokes.setup: Crew observes Leela's relationship
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg's cynical take on female attraction patterns
jokes.explanation: Dark observational humor that's somewhat dated. Not particularly character-specific for Zoidberg.

jokes.text: They were giving it away on the corner. Just like you, Leela.
jokes.setup: Alcazar explains where he got furniture
jokes.punchline: Cruel comparison of Leela to discarded items
jokes.explanation: Effective cruelty that establishes Alcazar's true nature. Good economy and structure.

jokes.text: You go, girl!
jokes.setup: Friend cheers after Alcazar's cruel insult
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate cheerleading for emotional abuse
jokes.explanation: Weak joke relying on dated catchphrase. Irony is obvious and lacks character specificity.

jokes.text: Jeez, Leela! Twice in one day? I'm not Superman!
jokes.setup: Leela asks if he can let Fry out of dungeon
jokes.punchline: Alcazar frames basic kindness as unreasonable sexual demand
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about emotional manipulation. Good character work showing Alcazar's gaslighting tactics.

jokes.text: If it's obvious to you, with your learning disability, of course I knew!
jokes.setup: Fry says Alcazar is obviously bad for her
jokes.punchline: Leela's cruel but accurate assessment of Fry's intelligence
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Leela's frustration. Backhanded logic is well-constructed.

jokes.text: Hey, everyone, jam a sock in your spit faucets.
jokes.setup: Alcazar wants everyone to be quiet
jokes.punchline: Crude but creative metaphor for mouths
jokes.explanation: Creative insult with good originality. 'Spit faucets' is memorably gross imagery.

jokes.text: No! No! Yes! I will!
jokes.setup: Alcazar proposes marriage publicly
jokes.punchline: Leela's internal conflict played out audibly
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Leela's internal struggle. Economy is excellent - conveys complex emotions quickly.

jokes.text: Animals go in the corner. Why didn't I think of that?
jokes.setup: Fry complains about dropping waste everywhere like an animal
jokes.punchline: Bender's helpful suggestion reveals even worse prison conditions
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of prison misery. Bender's practical advice makes degradation worse.

jokes.text: I don't know. For the first time in my life, I feel like I've stolen enough.
jokes.setup: Fry suggests stealing forbidden stuff
jokes.punchline: Bender having an existential crisis about his core personality trait
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment - Bender questioning his fundamental nature is genuinely funny and unexpected.

jokes.text: I love stealing I love taking things
jokes.setup: Bender snaps out of his existential crisis
jokes.punchline: Immediately returns to enthusiastic stealing with song
jokes.explanation: Good callback resolving Bender's brief character crisis. Returns to established personality with musical flair.

jokes.text: My God! Four identical castles! Each more identical than the last!
jokes.setup: Fry discovers multiple identical castles
jokes.punchline: Logical impossibility of increasing identicalness
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Fry logic - mathematically impossible comparison delivered with complete confidence. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: Saving a race of one-eyed monsters? Who could object to that?
jokes.setup: Standard wedding objection request
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic assessment of the situation
jokes.explanation: Functional ironic commentary but not particularly memorable or well-crafted.

jokes.text: Do you, Leela, copy and paste his response, till death do you part?
jokes.setup: Wedding vows being rushed through
jokes.punchline: Computer terminology applied to marriage ceremony
jokes.explanation: Decent tech humor applied to traditional ceremony. Not particularly character-specific but works for the setting.

jokes.text: Why you are so late for our wedding? And why you have only one eye?
jokes.setup: Another alien crashes the wedding
jokes.punchline: Reveals Alcazar is supposed to marry her too, with different eye configuration
jokes.explanation: Good plot revelation with escalating absurdity. Structure works well for the big reveal moment.

jokes.text: I needed five weirdos to scrub my five castles.
jokes.setup: Alcazar explains his elaborate deception
jokes.punchline: Reduces romantic relationships to domestic labor
jokes.explanation: Efficient revelation of his true motivations. Good economy in explaining the entire scheme.

jokes.text: He's a saint!
jokes.setup: Alcazar admits to exploiting everyone and selling voyeuristic access
jokes.punchline: Pig's enthusiastic endorsement of his exploiter
jokes.explanation: Good ironic contrast between abuse and praise. Economy is excellent but concept is fairly standard.

jokes.text: If you can change form, why not change it in the one place that counts?
jokes.setup: Leela confronts Alcazar about his shape-shifting abilities
jokes.punchline: Implies he's inadequately endowed despite magical powers
jokes.explanation: Good final insult with sexual implication. Effective revenge moment for Leela's character.

jokes.text: I mean, how many planets can there be?
jokes.setup: Professor reassures Leela about finding her home planet
jokes.punchline: Absurdly optimistic estimate in an infinite universe
jokes.explanation: Classic Professor obliviousness to scale. Good character integration with his tendency toward false optimism.

tmdb_overview: Leela meets Alkazar, who claims to be the only other remaining cyclops in the universe. But when Alkazar invites Leela back to his home planet and treats her like a slave, Fry suspects that something is amiss.
